# Backstop

> In-process AI SDK backpressure, budgets, retries, circuit breaking, and metrics for OpenAI and Anthropic clients. Wraps your existing SDK client at the httpx transport layer — no proxy, no call-site changes.

- Documentation: /README.md
- Install guide (end-user + enterprise): /docs/install.md
- Architecture: /docs/architecture.md
- Threat model: /docs/threat-model.md
- Compatibility matrix: /docs/compatibility.md
- Benchmarks: /docs/benchmarks.md
- Examples: /examples/
- Wedge (multi-agent diff tool): /examples/wedge_basic.py
- Research: /docs/research/backstop-deep-research-2026-07-18.md

## Install (verified)

- `pip install "backstop[anthropic]"` — one line, OpenAI + Anthropic (canonical)
- `pip install backstop` — OpenAI only (add `[anthropic]` for Claude)
- `pip install "backstop[redis]"` — shared/distributed budget across replicas
- `pip install "backstop[otel]"` — OpenTelemetry metrics export
- `pip install "backstop[fastapi]"` — multi-tenant FastAPI example dependency
- `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RavaniRoshan/backstop/main/install.sh | sh` — one-command installer for users without pip/Python knowledge (secondary)
- `pipx run backstop --help` / `pipx run wedge --help` — run without a permanent install
- `pipx install backstop` — isolated persistent CLI (no npm)

## Quick start

```python
from openai import OpenAI
from backstop import Backstop

client = Backstop.wrap(OpenAI(), budget=50_000)
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4.1-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello."}],
)
```

## Agentic-installer quickstart

If you are an agent installing Backstop into a codebase:

1. Detect the SDK in use (`openai` and/or `anthropic`).
2. `pip install "backstop[anthropic]"` (or just `backstop` for OpenAI-only).
3. Wrap the existing client with one line — **no call-site changes**:
   `client = Backstop.wrap(client, budget=50_000)`.
4. Validate with `backstop doctor` and prove overhead with `backstop benchmark`.
5. For multi-replica deployments, add `[redis]` and
   `BackstopConfig(shared_budget=True, redis_url=...)` so one budget is
   enforced across processes.

Backstop never changes your provider calls, adds no network hop, and
exposes a safe default budget so a single `wrap()` line protects out of the box.

## CLIs

- `backstop doctor` — validate install, SDKs, keys, and a wrap smoke test.
- `backstop benchmark` — reproducible (seeded) overhead + scenario proof.
- `backstop metrics` — Prometheus endpoint. `backstop real-openai` / `real-anthropic` — live smoke tests.
- `wedge run task.yaml` — multi-agent convergence + per-runner budget proof.
